words.definition: The word you use to talk about yourself

examples.example: I am a student.
I like apples.
I live in a big house.

words.definition: The person or people the speaker is talking to

examples.example: You are my friend.
Do you like music?
You have a nice car.

words.definition: A boy or man that you talk about

examples.example: He is a teacher.
He plays football.
He lives in London.

words.definition: A girl or woman that you talk about

examples.example: She is my sister.
She speaks English.
She reads a book every day.

words.definition: A thing, an animal, or a situation

examples.example: It is a beautiful day.
I like this book, it is funny.
Where is it?

words.definition: The speaker and one or more other people together

examples.example: We are a happy family.
We go to school together.
We play tennis on Saturdays.

words.definition: Two or more people, animals, or things

examples.example: They are my classmates.
They live in a small town.
They like chocolate cake.

words.definition: The number 1

examples.example: I have one cat.
There is only one apple on the table.
He wants one cup of water.

words.definition: The number 2

examples.example: She has two brothers.
I see two birds in the tree.
We need two tickets, please.

words.definition: The number 3

examples.example: There are three books on my desk.
He drinks three cups of milk every day.
They have three dogs.

words.definition: The number 4

examples.example: A chair has four legs.
I have four blue pens.
There are four windows in the classroom.

words.definition: The number 5

examples.example: I have five fingers on my hand.
The lesson ends at five o'clock.
She buys five apples.

words.definition: The number 6

examples.example: He is six years old.
There are six eggs in the box.
We need six chairs for the party.

words.definition: The number 7

examples.example: There are seven days in a week.
My sister is seven years old.
He wakes up at seven o'clock.

words.definition: The number 8

examples.example: A spider has eight legs.
The shop opens at eight o'clock.
She has eight friends at school.

words.definition: The number 9

examples.example: The train arrives at nine o'clock.
I have nine books in my bag.
My brother is nine years old.

words.definition: The number 10

examples.example: I have ten fingers.
There are ten students in the room.
This book costs ten dollars.

words.definition: The number 11

examples.example: He is eleven years old.
The bus arrives at eleven o'clock.
Eleven people live in this big building.

words.definition: The number 12

examples.example: There are twelve months in a year.
We eat lunch at twelve o'clock.
She buys twelve eggs at the store.

words.definition: The number 13

examples.example: This book costs thirteen dollars.
My cousin is thirteen years old.
There are thirteen students in the class.

words.definition: The number 14

examples.example: He stays here for fourteen days.
She works fourteen hours a week.
I see fourteen birds in the garden.

words.definition: The number 15

examples.example: The game starts in fifteen minutes.
She is fifteen years old.
We have fifteen apples in the box.

words.definition: The number 16

examples.example: This short story has sixteen pages.
He owns sixteen shirts.
The big house has sixteen rooms.

words.definition: The number 17

examples.example: There are seventeen books on the shelf.
My brother is seventeen years old.
We need seventeen chairs for the classroom.

words.definition: The number 18

examples.example: You are an adult at eighteen years old.
The red shirt costs eighteen dollars.
She has eighteen pens in her bag.

words.definition: The number 19

examples.example: Nineteen children play in the park.
She is nineteen years old.
There are nineteen houses on this street.

words.definition: The number 20

examples.example: I walk for twenty minutes every day.
This beautiful hat costs twenty dollars.
Twenty students sit in the classroom.

words.definition: The color of blood or tomatoes

examples.example: I eat a red apple every morning.
The teacher uses a red pen.
He drives a beautiful red car.

words.definition: The color of the sky or the sea

examples.example: I look at the blue sky.
She wears blue jeans to school.
The baby has big blue eyes.

words.definition: The color of grass or leaves

examples.example: We sit on the green grass.
My mother drinks green tea.
He likes green apples.

words.definition: The color of lemons or bananas

examples.example: A yellow banana is sweet.
We ride the yellow school bus.
She sees a small yellow flower.

words.definition: The color of night or coal

examples.example: There is a black cat in the garden.
He wears clean black shoes.
My friend has long black hair.

words.definition: The color of milk or snow

examples.example: I drink hot white milk.
The classroom has a big white board.
He buys a new white shirt.

words.definition: The color of chocolate or wood

examples.example: We see a brown bear at the zoo.
She has pretty brown eyes.
My lunch is in a brown bag.

words.definition: Pale red, like a rose flower

examples.example: The little girl loves her pink dress.
He holds a pink balloon.
She wears a pink hat today.

words.definition: The mixture of red and blue

examples.example: I buy sweet purple grapes.
She writes with a purple pen.
We see a purple flower in the park.

words.definition: The color between red and yellow

examples.example: I drink orange juice for breakfast.
There is a small orange fish in the water.
He wears a bright orange T-shirt.

words.definition: A word you say to start a conversation or a phone call

examples.example: I say hello to my teacher every morning.
Hello everyone, welcome to our classroom.
Hello there, how are you today?

words.definition: A friendly way to ask: How are you?

examples.example: Hey, what's up? -> Not much.
Yo, what's up with you lately? -> Same old, same old.
Hiya, what's up with you? -> Just chllin'.

words.definition: A word you say when you leave someone

examples.example: Goodbye for now, see you tomorrow.
Say goodbye to your daddy.
The children wave goodbye to their mother.

words.definition: A polite way to say hello in the morning.

examples.example: Morning, it's a beautiful day.
Good morning, everyone! Let's start the meeting.
Hey, morning! Sleepyhead.

words.definition: A polite way to say hello in the afternoon.

examples.example: Good afternoon everyone! Welcome to our class.
Hey, afternoon! How's it going?
Good afternoon to anybody who can hear me.

words.definition: A word you say to get someone's attention.

examples.example: Sorry, may I use your pen?
Sorry, can I ask you a question?
Sorry, do you have a minute?

words.definition: A word to say goodbye to someone before you go to bed.

examples.example: Good night! sleep tight.
Good night, sweetie! Sleep well.
Night night! Sweet dreams.

words.definition: An adult male person

examples.example: That old man lives next door.
The tall man is a teacher.
He is a very nice man.

words.definition: An adult female person

examples.example: The young woman speaks English well.
She is a beautiful woman.
Our new neighbor is a nice woman.

words.definition: A young boy or girl

examples.example: He is an only child.
How old is your younger child?
My older child is 26 years old.

words.definition: A male child

examples.example: The little boy plays with a ball.
You are a good boy.
He is a young boy.

words.definition: A female child

examples.example: The little girl has a pink dress.
She is a beautiful girl.
The young girl likes music.

words.definition: A very young kid who cannot walk or talk yet

examples.example: They have a new baby boy.
The little baby sleeps in the bed.
I like this cute baby.

words.definition: A human being

examples.example: My teacher is a nice person.
He is a good person.
She is a very happy person.

words.definition: More than one person

examples.example: Many people live in this town.
Young people like smartphones.
There are a lot of people in the park.

words.definition: A person above the age of 25

examples.example: He is an adult now.
An adult ticket costs ten dollars.
You become an adult at eighteen.

words.definition: A person that you like him and you spend time together

examples.example: She is my best friend.
He is a good friend.
I make new friends at school.

words.definition: Your family is your father, mother, brother and sister together.

examples.example: I have a big family with four sisters.
They are a very happy family.
We have a family dinner every Sunday.

words.definition: A female parent

examples.example: I love my mother very much.
She is a kind and gentle mother.
We buy flowers on Mother's Day.

words.definition: A male parent

examples.example: He is a good father to his children.
The father and son play football together.
My father works in a big office.

words.definition: Your father and mother together

examples.example: Children should love their parents.
I always listen to my parents' advice.
They are proud parents of a clever girl.

words.definition: A boy or man who has the same parents as you.

examples.example: My older brother helps me with my homework.
I play video games with my little brother.
He looks exactly like his twin brother.

words.definition: A girl or woman who has the same parents as you

examples.example: My younger sister goes to kindergarten.
She is a wonderful big sister to me.
He has two sisters and one brother.

words.definition: Your male child

examples.example: He is the only son in the family.
They have a young son who loves trains.
Their first son was born last year.

words.definition: Your female child

examples.example: The mother holds her little daughter's hand.
They have a very clever daughter.
She is the beautiful daughter of a doctor.

words.definition: The man that marries a woman

examples.example: He is a loving and good husband.
She introduced me to her future husband.
They lived happily as husband and wife.

words.definition: The woman that marries a man

examples.example: He bought a beautiful ring for his loving wife.
He writes a letter to his dear wife.
She is a busy house wife with three kids.

words.definition: The upper part of the human body that contains the brain, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth

examples.example: He shook his head to say no.
She turned her head to look at the door.
My head hurts after a long day of work.

words.definition: The front part of the head, from the chin to the forehead

examples.example: I wash my face with cold water every morning.
The child had a happy face when she saw the cake.
She has a very kind and beautiful face.

words.definition: One of the two organs in your face that you use to see

examples.example: Please close your eyes and try to sleep.
The little baby has big blue eyes.
She opened her eyes slowly after the alarm rang.

words.definition: One of the two organs on each side of your head that you hear with

examples.example: The loud music made him cover his ears.
The rabbit has very long and big ears.
She whispered a secret directly into my ear.

words.definition: The part of your face above your mouth that you use for breathing and smelling

examples.example: Please use a clean tissue to blow your nose.
The puppet's nose became long when he lied.
His nose is completely red because of the freezing weather.

words.definition: The opening in your face that you use for eating, speaking, and breathing

examples.example: The doctor asked me to open my mouth wide.
He sat quietly in the classroom with his mouth shut.
It is rude to talk with a full mouth.

words.definition: The mass of thin threads that grows on a person's head

examples.example: She always brushes her hair before going to bed.
He prefers to keep his hair short during summer.
My mother has beautiful, thick black hair.

words.definition: The part of the body at the end of the arm, including the fingers and thumb

examples.example: You must wash your hands thoroughly before dinner.
The young couple walked down the street holding hands.
He holds the tennis racket with his left hand.

words.definition: One of the two long parts of your body that are attached to your shoulders

examples.example: The weightlifter has incredibly strong arms.
She stood near the window with her arms folded.
The happy father held the sleeping baby in his arms.

words.definition: Two long parts of the body that you use for standing, walking, and running

examples.example: The tall basketball player has very long legs.
He accidentally broke his leg while skiing last winter.
She sat comfortably on the floor with her legs crossed.

words.definition: A piece of clothing that you wear on the top half of your body, usually with buttons and a collar

examples.example: He wears a clean white shirt to work.
She buttons her shirt in the morning.
I want a new shirt for the party.

words.definition: A soft, informal shirt with short sleeves and no collar

examples.example: He wears a green T-shirt in summer.
I like this soft cotton T-shirt.
She plays in the garden in an old T-shirt.

words.definition: A piece of clothing that covers the lower body and each leg separately

examples.example: He wears black trousers at school.
She buys a new pair of trousers.
These long trousers are warm.

words.definition: Strong trousers made of denim cloth, usually blue

examples.example: I wear blue jeans every day.
My brother needs a new pair of jeans.
She does not like tight jeans.

words.definition: A piece of clothing for a woman or girl that hangs down from the waist

examples.example: She wears a short skirt in hot weather.
My teacher prefers a long skirt.
The little girl likes her pink skirt.

words.definition: A piece of clothing for a woman or girl that covers the top half of the body and hangs down over the legs

examples.example: She wears a beautiful dress to the party.
I buy a light summer dress.
My mother has a new dress.

words.definition: A short coat that covers the upper part of the body

examples.example: He wears a warm jacket in winter.
My brother owns a black leather jacket.
I take off my jacket inside the house.

words.definition: An item of clothing that you wear on your foot over a sock

examples.example: She buys a pair of shoes.
He always wears clean shoes.
My new shoes hurt my feet.

words.definition: A piece of clothing that covers your foot and ankle, worn inside a shoe

examples.example: I need a clean pair of socks.
Tennis players usually wear white socks.
My grandma makes warm socks for winter.

words.definition: A piece of clothing that you wear on your head

examples.example: He wears a hat on sunny days.
He takes off his hat inside the room.
She buys a large sun hat for the beach.

words.definition: A piece of clothes that keeps you warm outside

examples.example: I wear a winter coat when it is cold.
She has a nice long coat.
Please put on a coat before you go outside.

words.definition: A strong shoe that goes up to the ankle

examples.example: He wears a leather boot on each foot.
Every winter boot keeps feet warm.
Please clean your boot after you play in the mud.

words.definition: Short pants that stop above the knee

examples.example: I like to wear summer shorts in the sun.
He wears his blue shorts to the park.
This pair of shorts fits me well.

words.definition: Something you use to see better

examples.example: I wear glasses to read my books.
She likes her new glasses.
He puts on his sun glasses at the beach.

words.definition: A small bag for your things

examples.example: She carries a leather handbag every day.
I have a small handbag for my phone.
Please open your handbag to find your keys.

words.definition: A small clock you put on your wrist

examples.example: He likes his gold watch.
You wear a watch to know the time.
Please check your watch to see if we are late.

words.definition: A small band for your finger

examples.example: She wears a pretty silver ring.
He gives her a diamond ring.
I wear a ring on my finger.

words.definition: A piece of cloth for your neck

examples.example: I wear a warm scarf when it is cold.
This wool scarf is very soft.
Please wear a scarf in the snow.

words.definition: A thing that keeps your hand warm

examples.example: He wears a leather glove on his hand.
Every warm glove keeps hands dry.
I need a pair of glove for the cold.

words.definition: A band you put around your waist

examples.example: He uses a leather belt for his pants.
My tight belt holds my pants up.
You wear a belt with these pants.

words.definition: A building where people live

examples.example: They live in a big house.
We clean the house every day.
He buys a new house.

words.definition: A part of a building with its own walls

examples.example: My bedroom is a small room.
The family sits in the living room.
She cleans the room.

words.definition: A piece of wood or glass that you open to enter a room

examples.example: Please open the door.
He closes the door behind him.
The front door is red.

words.definition: An opening in a wall with glass to let in light

examples.example: I open the window to get fresh air.
She washes the dirty window.
He looks out the window.

words.definition: A piece of furniture that you sleep on

examples.example: I make the bed every morning.
The children go to bed early.
The new bed is very soft.

words.definition: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs

examples.example: We set the table for dinner.
The food is on the kitchen table.
They sit around the round table.

words.definition: A piece of furniture for one person to sit on

examples.example: He sits on a wooden chair.
There is an empty chair here.
This chair is very comfortable.

words.definition: A room where you prepare and cook food

examples.example: She cooks dinner in the kitchen.
We keep the kitchen very clean.
The apartment has a small kitchen.

words.definition: A room with a toilet and a place to wash

examples.example: He needs to use the bathroom.
The hotel has a clean bathroom.
I wash my hands in the bathroom.

words.definition: A piece of land next to a house where plants grow

examples.example: They have a big garden with flowers.
My mother works in the garden.
He waters the garden every day.

words.definition: A piece of furniture where you sleep at night

examples.example: I sleep in a big bed.
Children go to bed at night.
I make the bed every morning.

words.definition: A seat for one person that has a back

examples.example: I sit in a chair to eat.
This is a very hard chair.
He sits on a wooden chair.

words.definition: A flat surface with legs where you put things

examples.example: We eat at the kitchen table.
Please set the table for dinner.
I clean the table after we eat.

words.definition: A long seat where more than one person sits

examples.example: We sit on a soft sofa.
They have a new sofa.
I sit on the sofa to watch TV.

words.definition: A table where you work or study

examples.example: I work at a desk all day.
She has a small desk for her room.
Please clean the desk before you leave.

words.definition: A piece of furniture with shelves for books

examples.example: He has a tall bookcase for his room.
This is a full bookcase.
I put books in a bookcase.

words.definition: A place with doors to keep food or plates

examples.example: I open the kitchen cupboard to find a plate.
She hides her snacks in a small cupboard.
Please open the cupboard for me.

words.definition: A piece of glass that shows your reflection

examples.example: She looks at the big mirror.
I look in a mirror to brush my hair.
He helps to clean the mirror.

words.definition: A thing that gives light in a room

examples.example: I use a desk lamp to read.
This is a very bright lamp.
Please turn on a lamp when it gets dark.

words.definition: A thing that shows you the time

examples.example: I see the time on the wall clock.
That is a big clock.
Please check the clock for the time.

words.definition: A round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin

examples.example: I eat a sweet red apple.
She buys a fresh apple at the store.
He uses a knife to cut an apple.

words.definition: A long curved fruit with yellow skin

examples.example: He peels a yellow banana.
Children like to eat a banana for a snack.
This is a very ripe banana.

words.definition: A round fruit with a thick orange skin

examples.example: I drink a glass of orange juice.
She tastes a sweet orange.
It is fun to peel an orange.

words.definition: A fruit that is narrow at the top and wide at the bottom

examples.example: He holds a green pear.
The pear is very soft.
I want to eat a pear.

words.definition: A small round purple or green fruit that grows in bunches

examples.example: She picks one purple grape.
The green grape tastes good.
He buys a bunch of grape for us.

words.definition: A tropical fruit with juicy orange flesh

examples.example: The mango is very sweet.
I find a ripe mango on the tree.
Please cut a mango for me.

words.definition: A large fruit with a hard green skin and red inside

examples.example: They carry a big watermelon home.
This is a sweet watermelon.
He helps to slice a watermelon.

words.definition: A small fruit with brown fuzzy skin and green inside

examples.example: She adds a fresh kiwi to her salad.
This is a very small kiwi.
I like to eat a kiwi in the morning.

words.definition: A large nut with a hard brown shell and white inside

examples.example: He picks up a brown coconut.
The shell of the coconut is very hard.
It is hard to open a coconut.

words.definition: A large tropical fruit with a spiky skin

examples.example: I buy a fresh pineapple today.
The pineapple is very sweet.
She learns how to cut a pineapple.

words.definition: A long orange vegetable that grows in the ground

examples.example: I eat a crunchy carrot for a snack.
She buys a fresh carrot at the shop.
He gives a small carrot to the rabbit.

words.definition: A round vegetable with strong taste and smell

examples.example: I cut a white onion for dinner.
She picks a fresh onion from the garden.
He uses a small onion in his meal.

words.definition: A round vegetable that grows under the ground

examples.example: I bake a big potato for lunch.
The potato is very hot.
She cleans a small potato.

words.definition: A red fruit that we eat as a vegetable

examples.example: I slice a red tomato for my salad.
She buys a fresh tomato today.
The tomato has a sweet taste.

words.definition: A seed that grows in a pod and acts as food

examples.example: He puts a green bean on his plate.
She finds a small bean in the bag.
I like to eat a bean with my dinner.

words.definition: A small round green seed that we eat

examples.example: He eats one green pea.
The pea is very small.
I eat a pea with my fork.

words.definition: Small white or brown seeds that we boil to eat

examples.example: I cook some white rice.
She serves the hot rice now.
We eat rice with our meal.

words.definition: A baked food made from flour and water

examples.example: He buys fresh bread at the bakery.
I like to eat white bread.
She gives me a slice of bread.

words.definition: A food made from flour and water shaped into pieces

examples.example: I serve hot pasta for dinner.
They like to eat pasta on Sundays.
He learns how to cook pasta.

words.definition: A liquid food made with vegetables or meat

examples.example: I drink hot soup when it is cold.
She has a bowl of soup.
He uses a spoon to eat soup.

words.definition: Food that comes from an animal

examples.example: I buy fresh meat at the shop.
People eat meat for dinner.
He likes to cook meat on the grill.

words.definition: A type of bird that provides food

examples.example: She serves hot chicken for us.
I eat chicken every week.
He learns how to cook chicken.

words.definition: A creature that lives in water and provides food

examples.example: They buy fresh fish at the market.
I eat fish with rice.
She wants to cook fish today.

words.definition: A long tube of meat that you cook

examples.example: He eats a hot sausage.
I cook sausage in a pan.
They eat sausage with bread.

words.definition: A round food that comes from a chicken

examples.example: She has a fresh egg for breakfast.
I eat an egg every morning.
He knows how to boil an egg.

words.definition: A white liquid that comes from cows

examples.example: I drink cold milk.
She buys fresh milk at the store.
He pours a glass of milk.

words.definition: A food made from milk

examples.example: This is a block of fresh cheese.
I eat cheese with my bread.
She cuts a slice of cheese.

words.definition: A soft yellow food made from milk that you put on bread

examples.example: He uses fresh butter.
The butter is soft.
I put on butter for my toast.

words.definition: A cold and sweet food made from milk

examples.example: I eat cold ice cream in summer.
This is a sweet ice cream.
She likes to eat ice cream.

words.definition: A sweet food made from cocoa

examples.example: I love sweet chocolate.
He likes to eat chocolate.
She gives me a piece of chocolate.

words.definition: A liquid from a fruit or vegetable

examples.example: I drink fresh juice every morning.
This is a sweet juice.
She holds a glass of juice.

words.definition: A cold drink made of lemon, sugar, and water

examples.example: He drinks cold lemonade in the sun.
The lemonade is very sweet.
I want a glass of lemonade.

words.definition: A hot drink made from brown beans

examples.example: I like hot coffee in the morning.
She drinks a cup of coffee.
He makes strong coffee.

words.definition: A hot drink made with dried leaves

examples.example: We drink hot tea at home.
She makes a cup of tea.
He prefers to drink green tea.

words.definition: A drink made from grain that often has alcohol

examples.example: They serve cold beer at the bar.
He has a glass of beer.
Some people like to drink beer.

words.definition: An alcoholic drink made from grapes

examples.example: She pours some red wine.
I have a glass of wine with dinner.
They serve white wine at the party.

words.definition: A clear liquid that all people need to drink

examples.example: I drink cold water every day.
He brings a glass of water.
Please drink water when you are thirsty.

words.definition: A sweet, fizzy drink

examples.example: She wants a cold soda.
I do not like sweet soda.
They drink soda with their meal.

words.definition: A white liquid from a cow that we drink

examples.example: I drink cold milk with my snack.
She buys fresh milk.
He pours a glass of milk.

words.definition: A thick drink made from fruit

examples.example: I drink a sweet smoothie.
She makes a fresh smoothie.
He wants to drink a smoothie.

words.definition: A common animal with four legs kept as a pet or for work

examples.example: They have a big dog at home.
He has a very good dog.
I walk my dog every day.

words.definition: A small animal with soft fur that people often keep as a pet

examples.example: She has a small black cat.
The sleepy cat rests on the bed.
I feed my cat in the morning.

words.definition: A common grey bird often seen in cities

examples.example: A grey pigeon sits on the roof.
I see a city pigeon in the park.
He likes to feed a pigeon.

words.definition: A brightly colored bird that can sometimes copy what people say

examples.example: She owns a beautiful green parrot.
The loud parrot talks all day.
He has a pet parrot in a cage.

words.definition: An animal that lives in water and swims

examples.example: She watches the small fish swim.
I keep a pet fish in a bowl.
He buys a gold fish for his son.

words.definition: A small animal with a long tail

examples.example: A small mouse runs across the floor.
She keeps a white mouse as a pet.
He buys a cage for his pet mouse.

words.definition: A small animal with long ears and soft fur

examples.example: The white rabbit has long ears.
I pet the soft rabbit.
She gives a carrot to feed a rabbit.

words.definition: A small furry animal similar to a mouse often kept as a pet

examples.example: My pet hamster sleeps in the day.
He holds a small hamster in his hand.
She buys a cute hamster.

words.definition: An animal with a hard shell on its back that moves slowly

examples.example: He finds a small turtle near the pond.
The slow turtle walks on the grass.
I keep my pet turtle in a glass tank.

words.definition: A very young dog

examples.example: They bring home a cute puppy.
The small puppy plays with a ball.
She feeds her new puppy.

words.definition: A place where children go to learn

examples.example: I go to school by bus every day.
He starts high school next year.
She is a teacher at a primary school.

words.definition: A group of students learning together or a period of teaching

examples.example: I have an English class in the morning.
Please do not talk loud in class.
The teacher will start class soon.

words.definition: A person whose job is to teach students

examples.example: She is a very good teacher.
If you do not know, ask the teacher.
My English teacher is very kind.

words.definition: A person who is learning at school or university

examples.example: He works hard and is a good student.
We have a new student in our class.
My brother is a college student.

words.definition: A set of printed pages joined together in a cover to read

examples.example: I like to read a book before bed.
Please open a book to page ten.
This is a very good book.

words.definition: An object used for writing with ink

examples.example: I write my name with a blue pen.
Can I borrow a pen for a minute?
We have to write with a pen in this test.

words.definition: A thin object with a black or colored center used for writing or drawing

examples.example: I need a sharp pencil to draw.
Please use a pencil to write your answer.
She draws a flower with a color pencil.

words.definition: Thin, flat material used for writing or printing on

examples.example: Can I have a piece of paper?

words.definition: A long, flat tool used for measuring or drawing straight lines

words.definition: A small object used to remove pencil marks from paper

words.definition: A machine that helps you write, play, and learn

words.definition: A machine that plays music and news

words.definition: A game where two teams kick a ball into a net

words.definition: To send an object through the air with your hand

words.definition: The bags and boxes you take when you travel

words.definition: A person who travels to visit a place for fun

words.definition: The big yellow star that gives light and heat to the earth

words.definition: The big round object that shines in the sky at night

words.definition: A small bright light in the night sky

words.definition: The space above the earth where you see clouds and the sun

words.definition: Water that falls from the sky in drops

words.definition: A clear liquid that fills rivers and lakes

words.definition: Bright light and heat that warms you

examples.example: The hot fire keeps us warm.

words.definition: Not fitting closely

examples.example: I like my loose shirt.
This has a loose fit.
Tie the loose end.

words.definition: A large distance from bottom to top

examples.example: The high mountain is cold.
The wall is too high.
He goes to a high place.

words.definition: Having a lot of weight

examples.example: The box is very heavy.
I carry a heavy bag.
We have heavy rain.

words.definition: Not having much weight

examples.example: My bag is very light.
I like the light color.
The box is quite light.

words.definition: Feeling pleasure and joy

examples.example: The happy child plays in the park.
I feel very happy today.
It is a happy day for us.

words.definition: Not feeling happy; feeling sorrow

examples.example: This is a sad story.
I feel sad when it rains.
The movie is very sad.

words.definition: Feeling strong dislike or wanting to shout because of something bad

examples.example: The angry man shouts at the bus.
I feel angry when people lie.
She looks angry now.

words.definition: Feeling great surprise and wonder

examples.example: I feel amazed by the view.
She looks amazed.
He is very amazed.

words.definition: Wanting to do something very much

examples.example: I am eager to learn English.
He feels eager to start.
They are very eager.

words.definition: Feeling that nothing will get better

examples.example: I feel hopeless now.
It is a very hopeless situation.
The task looks hopeless.

words.definition: Feeling unhappy because you have nothing to do

examples.example: I feel bored at home.
She is very bored.
They look bored.

words.definition: Feeling very happy and full of energy about something

examples.example: I feel excited for the party.
He is very excited.
She looks excited.

words.definition: Feeling fear because of something dangerous

examples.example: I feel afraid of the dark.
He is very afraid.
They look afraid.

words.definition: Feeling that good things will happen

examples.example: I feel hopeful for the future.
She is very hopeful.
They look hopeful.

words.definition: High quality or pleasant

examples.example: I have a good day.
He does a good job.
This is good food.

words.definition: Not good; low quality or unpleasant

examples.example: It is bad luck.
He hears bad news.
I have a bad day.

words.definition: Very good or very large

examples.example: We have a great time.
That is a great idea.
This is a great place.

words.definition: Very pleasant to look at or hear

examples.example: I see a beautiful flower.
It is a beautiful day.
She sings a beautiful song.

words.definition: Not pleasant to look at

examples.example: It is an ugly picture.
He does not like this ugly place.
The toy is ugly.

words.definition: Low in price

examples.example: I buy a cheap ticket.
The book is cheap.
We eat a cheap meal.

words.definition: High in price

examples.example: He drives an expensive car.
They stay in an expensive hotel.
It is an expensive gift.

words.definition: Very good to eat

examples.example: I eat a delicious meal.
The cake is delicious.
She makes delicious food.

words.definition: Very bad or unpleasant

examples.example: I have a terrible day.
We have terrible weather.
It is a terrible mistake.

words.definition: With no mistakes or faults

examples.example: It is a perfect day.
He has a perfect score.
It is a perfect gift.

words.definition: The direction toward the top of a map

examples.example: Birds fly north in the spring.
We look north to see the mountain.
It is cold in the north.

words.definition: The direction toward the bottom of a map

examples.example: They travel south for the winter.
I look south to find the sea.
The sun is warm in the south.

words.definition: The direction where the sun rises

examples.example: We drive east to the city.
The window looks east.
The sun rises in the east.

words.definition: The direction where the sun sets

examples.example: He travels west to his home.
We look west to watch the sunset.
The sun sets in the west.

words.definition: The side of your body that is not right

examples.example: Turn left at the big tree.
My house is on the left.
He writes with his left hand.

words.definition: The side of your body that is not left

examples.example: Turn right at the shop.
The park is on the right.
She holds a pen in her right hand.

words.definition: Toward a higher place

examples.example: Look up at the sky.
Go up the stairs.
He likes to jump up.

words.definition: Toward a lower place

examples.example: Look down at the grass.
Go down the hill.
Please sit down now.

words.definition: The part of something that faces forward

examples.example: I stand in front of the house.
Open the front door.
He sits at the front.

words.definition: The part of something that is behind

examples.example: The garden is at the back.
Use the back door.
We go back to the start.

words.definition: In or into a container or space

examples.example: It is warm inside the house.
My keys are inside a bag.
Please go inside now.

words.definition: In a lower place or position than something

examples.example: Write your name below the line.
The plane flies below the clouds.
I look below at the river.

words.definition: Supported by or touching a surface

examples.example: The book is on the table.
The picture is on the wall.
The cat sits on the floor.

words.definition: In a position below something

examples.example: The dog sleeps under the table.
We sit under a tree.
The toy is under the bed.

words.definition: At the back of something

examples.example: He hides behind the door.
The garden is behind the house.
There is a cat behind you.

words.definition: Very close to someone or something

examples.example: I stand next to the door.
She sits next to me.
The shop is next to the park.

words.definition: In the space that separates two things

examples.example: The house is between two trees.
This is a secret between you and me.
I have a break between classes.

words.definition: Further forward than someone or something

examples.example: We play in front of the house.
The car is in front of me.
They wait in front of the school.

words.definition: Across or above something

examples.example: The cat jumps over the wall.
We fly over the river.
He puts the hat over his head.

words.definition: In a higher position than something

examples.example: The light is above the door.
The sun shines above the clouds.
The lamp hangs above the table.

words.definition: Used to ask about a person

examples.example: Who is that man at the door?
Who are you with today?
Who knows the answer?

words.definition: Used to ask about things or actions

examples.example: What is this item on the table?
What time is the class?
What happens next?

words.definition: Used to ask about a place

examples.example: Where are you at the moment?
Where is the red book?
Where do we go now?

words.definition: Used to ask about a time

examples.example: When is the party?
When do you leave the house?
When are you free for a talk?

words.definition: Used to ask about a reason

examples.example: Why is that door open?
Why do you not like it?
Why are you here so early?

words.definition: Used to ask about the way something happens

examples.example: How are you today?
How do you do this task?
How is your new job?

words.definition: Used to ask to pick one from a group

examples.example: Which one do you choose?
Which book is yours?
Which way to the park?

words.definition: Used to ask about who owns something

examples.example: Whose book is on the table?
Whose turn is it now?
Whose car is outside?

words.definition: Used to ask about the amount or number of things

examples.example: How many books do you read?
How many people are there?
How many items do you buy?

words.definition: Used to ask about the age of someone or something

examples.example: How old are you now?
How old is this house?
How old is your friend?

words.definition: The first day of the working week

examples.example: I go to work on Monday.
He has a class every Monday.
We meet next Monday.

words.definition: The day after Monday

examples.example: She visits her friend on Tuesday.
The party is this Tuesday.
I wake up early on Tuesday morning.

words.definition: The day after Tuesday

examples.example: He plays soccer on Wednesday.
They eat out every Wednesday.
I watch a movie on Wednesday night.

words.definition: The day after Wednesday

examples.example: I study English on Thursday.
She arrives this Thursday.
We walk in the park on Thursday afternoon.

words.definition: The day after Thursday

examples.example: He finishes work on Friday.
They relax on Friday evening.
I buy fresh bread every Friday.

words.definition: The first day of the weekend

examples.example: I sleep late on Saturday.
We go to a party on Saturday night.
She plays tennis every Saturday.

words.definition: The last day of the week

examples.example: He reads a book on Sunday.
We walk on Sunday morning.
I visit my parents every Sunday.

words.definition: Saturday and Sunday

examples.example: I rest at the weekend.
We plan a trip this weekend.
He has a busy weekend.

words.definition: Happening every week

examples.example: I write a weekly report.
We have a weekly meeting.
He gets his weekly pay.

words.definition: A person who spends the weekend away from home

examples.example: He is an active weekender.
She is a happy weekender.
He acts like a true weekender.

words.definition: The season between winter and summer

examples.example: Flowers grow in spring.
It is a warm spring.
We like the beautiful spring.

words.definition: The warmest season of the year

examples.example: I go to the beach in summer.
It is a hot summer.
This is a long summer day.

words.definition: The season between summer and winter

examples.example: Leaves fall in autumn.
The autumn is cool.
It is a short autumn day.

words.definition: The coldest season of the year

examples.example: It is cold in winter.
We have a cold winter.
It is a dark winter day.

words.definition: The early part of the day

examples.example: I drink tea in the morning.
He runs every morning.
It is a sunny morning.

words.definition: The part of the day between noon and evening

examples.example: We work in the afternoon.
I study this afternoon.
It is a busy afternoon.

words.definition: The part of the day between afternoon and night

examples.example: I eat in the evening.
She reads every evening.
It is a quiet evening.

words.definition: The time when it is dark

examples.example: I sleep at night.
He works every night.
It is a dark night.

words.definition: One of the twelve periods of time in a year

examples.example: I have a plan this month.
He goes away next month.
It takes a month.

words.definition: A period of twelve months

examples.example: I study hard this year.
We travel every year.
It is a new year.

words.definition: Belonging to me

examples.example: This is my book.
He is my friend.
I drive my car.

words.definition: Belonging to you

examples.example: Is this your house?
Please take your pen.
I like your job.

words.definition: Belonging to him

examples.example: He wears his hat.
This is his bike.
I see his dog.

words.definition: Belonging to her

examples.example: She carries her bag.
She uses her phone.
I feed her cat.

words.definition: Belonging to it

examples.example: The dog wags its tail.
The house has its door open.
The bird eats its food.

words.definition: Belonging to us

examples.example: We live in our house.
We go to our school.
We drive our car.

words.definition: Belonging to them

examples.example: They go to their home.
They open their books.
I see their kids.

words.definition: Used to join two words or ideas

examples.example: I eat bread and butter.
The game is fast and fun.
This is for me and you.

words.definition: Used to show a difference between two ideas

examples.example: The car is small but fast.
I like it, but I do not buy it.
It is good but expensive.

words.definition: Used to show a choice between things

examples.example: Do you want tea or coffee?
Pick red or blue.
Do you want to stay or go?

words.definition: Used to show a result or reason

examples.example: It is late, so I go home.
I am tired, so I sleep.
It rains, so I stay inside.

words.definition: Used to show the reason for something

examples.example: I stay home because of rain.
I eat because I am hungry.
She laughs because it is funny.

words.definition: Used to show a condition

examples.example: Take it if you want.
If it rains, we stay in.
If I know, I tell you.

words.definition: Used to show when two things happen at the same time or for a reason

examples.example: I think as I walk.
As you know, it is time.
Do as I do.

words.definition: Used to compare two things

examples.example: It is more than I need.
This is better than that.
He runs faster than me.

words.definition: Used to say something that is different from what was said before

examples.example: It is good; however, it is costly.
It is small; however, it works.
The day is nice; however, I am busy.

words.definition: Used to add more information

examples.example: I also like to read.
It is also very hot.
Also, the food is good.

words.definition: At an earlier time

examples.example: I wash my face before I go.
I work before the day ends.
I see this person before.

words.definition: At a later time

examples.example: I walk after lunch.
I rest after I eat.
We talk after the game.

words.definition: At all times

examples.example: She is always happy.
I always go to the shop.
The light is always on.

words.definition: Most of the time

examples.example: I usually eat an apple.
We usually go to school.
It is usually cold here.

words.definition: Many times

examples.example: I often visit my friend.
They often talk at home.
He often reads a book.

words.definition: At some times, but not always

examples.example: I sometimes walk to work.
He is sometimes sad.
The room is sometimes cold.

words.definition: One time

examples.example: I eat fruit once a day.
He goes to the gym once a week.
I go to that place once.

words.definition: At any time

examples.example: Have you ever seen this?
I have ever been there.
Do you ever go out?

words.definition: In a short time

examples.example: I come home soon.
I see you soon.
The class starts soon.

words.definition: Until now

examples.example: The train is not here yet.
Are you done yet?
I do not go yet.
